spec_match.rationale: Chromium-catches-up feature — the spec already documents the behaviour, so the value of the demo is the interop story. The concept exercises one corner but the before/after comparison isn't always crisp.

interactive.rationale: Has 2 interactive control(s) wired to JS but the surface is thin — a single button or readout is barely past the static-card threshold.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Concept page lede restates the feature-index blurb's key promise (overlap on element, engine, hover, pointerenter).

edge_cases.rationale: Demo runs the happy path; no visible feature detection, error handling, or no-support fallback in the script.

references.rationale: The concept page now includes the required canonical ChromeStatus self-heal link https://chromestatus.com/feature/6266812908175360.

design_system.rationale: All colours come from the shared CSS variables and the layout follows the stage + controls editorial pattern.

openQuestions.title: Cover the spec aspect: trace of the event sequence in a live DOM mutation
openQuestions.detail: The interoperable-pointer-and-mouse-boundary-events-after-dom-changes feature's headline framing — After a pointer target's removal, boundary events (over/out/enter/leave) target the nearest still-attached ancestor — interop change with Firefox/WebKit — calls out "trace of the event sequence in a live DOM mutation". This concept page does not exercise that, so the spec story is incomplete from this page alone. Either extend this demo or land a sibling concept dedicated to it.

openQuestions.title: Cover the spec aspect: before-after difference (showing what Chrome used to do)
openQuestions.detail: The interoperable-pointer-and-mouse-boundary-events-after-dom-changes feature's headline framing — After a pointer target's removal, boundary events (over/out/enter/leave) target the nearest still-attached ancestor — interop change with Firefox/WebKit — calls out "before-after difference (showing what Chrome used to do)". This concept page does not exercise that, so the spec story is incomplete from this page alone. Either extend this demo or land a sibling concept dedicated to it.

openQuestions.title: Add real controls so the demo isn't a styled code card
openQuestions.detail: Concept pages are supposed to be hands-on. With only 2 button(s), 0 input(s), 0 select(s), 0 range slider(s), the surface is thin. Add at least one slider, picker, or interactive readout so visitors can actually probe the API from the page.

openQuestions.title: Cover at least one realistic failure mode
openQuestions.detail: Add feature detection at the top of the script and surface the result. Handle the permission-denied / API-rejection case in the catch branch with a visible message. If the feature is flag-gated or origin-trial, show explicitly what the page does when the API is absent.

summary: dom-update-replay under interoperable-pointer-and-mouse-boundary-events-after-dom-changes: 2 pass / 3 partial / 1 fail. Interactive surface is thin, design system clean, spec story is partial — see open questions for the angles still missing. ChromeStatus reference link resolved.
